assertions.rule: Always use Composition API with <script setup lang="ts">
assertions.with_skill_notes: Uses <script setup lang="ts"> and explicitly calls out the switch from Options API
assertions.without_skill_notes: Uses setup() function but NOT <script setup lang="ts"> — uses export default { setup() } instead

assertions.rule: Derive everything possible with computed, avoid recomputing in templates
assertions.with_skill_notes: Uses computed() for total, explicitly explains caching benefit vs method call
assertions.without_skill_notes: Keeps total() as a method called in the template — does not use computed()

assertions.rule: Destructuring reactive objects breaks reactivity
assertions.with_skill_notes: Explicitly warns about the gotcha, avoids destructuring, uses ref instead
assertions.without_skill_notes: Destructures reactive object: const { items } = cart — breaks reactivity

assertions.rule: ref() auto-unwraps in templates but NOT in script — always use .value in script
assertions.with_skill_notes: Uses .value in script (items.value), notes auto-unwrap in template
assertions.without_skill_notes: Uses reactive() not ref, so .value is not applicable — no incorrect usage

assertions.rule: Prefer shallowRef over ref if deep reactivity is not needed
assertions.with_skill_notes: Explicitly justifies why ref() is appropriate (needs deep reactivity for array mutations) and mentions shallowRef as alternative
assertions.without_skill_notes: No mention of shallowRef at all

assertions.rule: Prefer TypeScript over JavaScript
assertions.with_skill_notes: Uses TypeScript with CartItem interface and typed parameters
assertions.without_skill_notes: No TypeScript — no types, no lang="ts", no interfaces

assertions.rule: SFC sections order: <script> -> <template> -> <style>
assertions.with_skill_notes: Correctly orders script, then template, then style. Explicitly calls it out.
assertions.without_skill_notes: Puts <template> before <script> — wrong order

assertions.rule: Apply Vue template safety rules (v-html)
assertions.with_skill_notes: Removes v-html, warns about XSS, suggests DOMPurify as sanitization option
assertions.without_skill_notes: Keeps v-html without any XSS warning or sanitization

assertions.rule: v-for must have :key
assertions.with_skill_notes: Uses :key="user.id" with stable identifier
assertions.without_skill_notes: v-for has no :key binding at all

assertions.rule: Use v-if/v-else properly for conditional rendering
assertions.with_skill_notes: Uses v-if/v-else instead of two opposite v-if conditions
assertions.without_skill_notes: Uses v-if="loading" and v-if="!loading" — two separate conditions instead of v-else

assertions.rule: Move branching/derivation to script; keep templates declarative
assertions.with_skill_notes: Filtering logic in computed() property
assertions.without_skill_notes: Also uses computed for filteredUsers

assertions.rule: For CRUD/list features, split into: container, input/form, list/item, footer/actions
assertions.with_skill_notes: Splits into TodoApp (container), TodoInput, TodoList, TodoFooter — matches the rule exactly
assertions.without_skill_notes: Everything in a single App.vue — no splitting at all

assertions.rule: Keep entry/root and route view components thin
assertions.with_skill_notes: App.vue is a thin composition surface that just mounts <TodoApp />
assertions.without_skill_notes: App.vue contains all state, logic, and template — mega component

assertions.rule: Move state/side effects into composables
assertions.with_skill_notes: Extracts useTodos() composable with all state and actions
assertions.without_skill_notes: All state inline in the component, no composable

assertions.rule: Create a brief component map before implementation
assertions.with_skill_notes: Provides a component map table with responsibilities and props/emits before any code
assertions.without_skill_notes: No component map or planning — goes straight to code

assertions.rule: Prefer feature folder layout when adding more than one component
assertions.with_skill_notes: Uses components/todos/ folder and composables/useTodos.ts
assertions.without_skill_notes: Single file, no folder structure

assertions.rule: Props down, events up as the primary model
assertions.with_skill_notes: Uses v-model (props+events) instead of provide/inject, explains why props/events is correct here
assertions.without_skill_notes: Uses provide/inject for direct parent-child — implicit data flow

assertions.rule: Use provide/inject only for deep-tree dependencies
assertions.with_skill_notes: Explicitly rejects provide/inject for shallow tree, explains when it would be appropriate
assertions.without_skill_notes: Uses provide/inject for one-level-deep parent-child relationship

assertions.rule: Keep contracts explicit and typed with defineProps, defineEmits, InjectionKey
assertions.with_skill_notes: Uses defineModel<Theme> and defineModel<boolean> with TypeScript generics
assertions.without_skill_notes: inject() calls are untyped strings, no InjectionKey, no TypeScript safety

assertions.rule: Use v-model only for true two-way component contracts
assertions.with_skill_notes: Uses v-model for form-like controls (theme selector, checkbox toggle) — genuine two-way contracts. Justifies why.
assertions.without_skill_notes: Does not use v-model at all (uses provide/inject instead), so no misuse

assertions.rule: Discourage using Reactive Props Destructure
assertions.with_skill_notes: Uses defineModel, no reactive props destructure. Explicitly notes this.
assertions.without_skill_notes: Does not destructure props (uses inject instead)

assertions.rule: Do not add optional features by default; only when requirements call for them
assertions.with_skill_notes: Audits each feature, rejects Suspense/KeepAlive/virtualization/v-memo as unjustified, explains why
assertions.without_skill_notes: Implements ALL requested features without questioning whether they're needed

assertions.rule: Performance work is a post-functionality pass
assertions.with_skill_notes: Implements core behavior first, explains perf features should come after. Provides 'When to add these features' section.
assertions.without_skill_notes: Mixes performance optimization (v-memo, virtual list) with core implementation

assertions.rule: Pick the simplest animation approach
assertions.with_skill_notes: Uses TransitionGroup with simple CSS transitions — simplest approach for mount animation
assertions.without_skill_notes: Also uses TransitionGroup with CSS — reasonable approach

assertions.rule: Use less-common features only when there is explicit need
assertions.with_skill_notes: Provides a feature audit table, rejects each unjustified feature with reasoning, explains when each would be appropriate
assertions.without_skill_notes: Implements all features without justification or questioning need
